commonMistakes: Mutating props or state instead of producing the next value.
Adding state or an Effect for a value that can be derived during render.
Using global state before identifying the real owner and consumers.
Optimizing renders without a measured user-facing problem.

contentAuthority.canonical: New JS2Next Release 1 curriculum and independently authored content.
contentAuthority.courseTranscripts: Coverage and sequencing research only; never learner-facing authority.
contentAuthority.legacyProjectContent: Supplementary internal reference only; excluded from runtime unless explicitly re-authored and approved.

learningObjectives.text: Compare libraries versus frameworks using a new example and justify the result.
Explain common react ecosystem categories using a new example and justify the result.
Explain how to evaluate third-party tools using a new example and justify the result.

mentalModel: Draw a decision map: **Libraries versus frameworks** → **Common React ecosystem categories** → **How to evaluate third-party tools**. Add branches where the learner must choose an alternative and label the evidence for the choice.

choices.feedback: Libraries versus frameworks is best understood as follows: A library provides focused capabilities while an application chooses broader structure; a framework supplies more conventions and lifecycle.
choices.label: A library provides focused capabilities while an application chooses broader structure; a framework supplies more conventions and lifecycle.

choices.feedback: This does not match the lesson's model. Reconsider the role of Libraries versus frameworks.
choices.label: UI state describes current interaction. Remote state is server-owned and adds asynchronous freshness, caching, and synchronization concerns.

choices.feedback: This does not match the lesson's model. Reconsider the role of Libraries versus frameworks.
choices.label: Redux reducers calculate next state from current state and an action. They must be predictable and side-effect free.

choices.feedback: This does not match the lesson's model. Reconsider the role of Libraries versus frameworks.
choices.label: Different element types replace a subtree; matching types are updated. Position and keys determine identity and state preservation.

questions.explanation: Libraries versus frameworks is best understood as follows: A library provides focused capabilities while an application chooses broader structure; a framework supplies more conventions and lifecycle.
questions.hint: Focus on what the concept changes, enables, or constrains—not only on its syntax.

choices.feedback: Common React ecosystem categories is best understood as follows: The ecosystem includes routing, styling, forms, global state, server-state tools, testing, component libraries, and full-stack frameworks.
choices.label: The ecosystem includes routing, styling, forms, global state, server-state tools, testing, component libraries, and full-stack frameworks.

choices.feedback: The reliable sequence starts from the requirement, applies a known rule, observes behavior, and improves the solution afterward.
choices.label: First identify the requirement, then apply the relevant rule from Libraries versus frameworks, inspect the result, and only then refactor or optimize.

choices.feedback: Engineering judgment balances clarity and cost. The concept should serve the requirement rather than become an end in itself.

sections.body: A library provides focused capabilities while an application chooses broader structure; a framework supplies more conventions and lifecycle.
sections.title: Libraries versus frameworks

summary: React Ecosystem Overview connects Libraries versus frameworks, Common React ecosystem categories, How to evaluate third-party tools.
Use the topic to make behavior and decisions explicit rather than memorizing isolated syntax.
Verify understanding by explaining a new example and answering the topic quiz without looking back.
title: React Ecosystem Overview

topicQuiz.failurePolicy: Show explanations, add missed objectives to local review, and allow immediate or later retry.

whyThisMatters: The ideas in react ecosystem overview recur throughout later React work, reducing memorization and debugging later. It belongs to the **How React Renders** module.
